metric_value: Signed as 'Founder and Chief Research Officer', Aberdeen Group.
Aberdeen rated CA's analyst-relations team as 'at the top of the industry'; Debra Cattani leading, Janice Thomas providing 'superlative' day-to-day support.
CA customers 'more satisfied today than at any time since I started following CA in 1992' — Kastner nine-year perspective.
Long-standing CA reputation problem with customers was substantially reduced by mid-2001.
Uninformed press still operating from 'old, bad CA' mindset; Kastner recommends quietly focused PR campaign to mitigate over time.
'The Ranger situation is a non-starter with customers' — Kastner predicting Ranger would fail to gain customer traction in the CA proxy fight.
Ranger Governance lost the August 2001 CA proxy contest (~73% of shareholders voted with CA management).

The '3x6 theme' (six business values across three market segments) produced cohesive branding across executive presentations, show floor, press conferences; 'something for everyone'; energized sales force and channel partners.
'The 'new CA' gathering momentum — and I mean that in the physics sense.' Kastner used the physics metaphor (mass x velocity) deliberately.
'I look forward to speaking with you soon' — direct-access relationship between Aberdeen CRO and CA CEO.
